arts.ttl: Arne Slot heaps praise on “outstanding” Liverpool star who changed the game at 1-1
arts.sum: Liverpool drew with Newcastle in a 3-3 thriller at St James’ Park on Wednesday night and the home side may have won the match if it was not for Mohamed Salah. The Egyptian superstar has been in phenomenal form during the opening part of the 2024/25 campaign with the winger’s goals playing a vital role […] The post Arne Slot heaps praise on “outstanding” Liverpool star who changed the game at 1-1 appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Premier League forced to release two VAR statements in Newcastle vs Liverpool chaos
arts.sum: Latest Liverpool news as the Premier League explain controversial refereeing calls against Newcastle United with VAR confusion

arts.ttl: Arne Slot acknowledges ‘mixed feelings’ after Liverpool let late lead slip at Newcastle
arts.sum: The Reds were behind twice but also led with only a minute left on the clock

arts.ttl: Arne Slot issues Mo Salah demand and slams Liverpool 'mistakes' in Newcastle draw
arts.sum: Arne Slot saw Liverpool held to a point by Newcastle in one of the games of the Premier League season, with the Reds boss left frustrated that his side threw away a win

arts.ttl: Newcastle United player ratings as two stars scoop 9/10 on crazy night vs Liverpool
arts.sum: Mo Salah's double looked like it had won it for the Reds but Newcastle refused to throw in the towel and earned a point through Fabian Schar. Battled hard but undone by some moments of quality from Liverpool's attack, not least Salah's stunning turn and shot.

arts.ttl: Eddie Howe reflects on what disappointed him in Newcastle United's 3-3 draw with Liverpool
arts.sum: On the game, Howe reflected: "Generally I was very pleased with the performance, we were much better attacking and we had really good energy. "It was a really good quality performance against probably the best team we have played against this season.

arts.ttl: Manchester City and Arsenal win as leaders Liverpool held at Newcastle
arts.sum: Liverpool’s draw allowed Arsenal to cut the gap at the top of the table to seven points.

arts.ttl: Howe 'gutted' Newcastle did not win 'great game'
arts.sum: Newcastle boss Eddie Howe says he is "gutted" not to win a "great game" as his side let slip their early lead against Liverpool, before snatching a late equaliser at St James' Park.

arts.ttl: Fabian Schär stuns Liverpool to snatch late Newcastle equaliser in thriller
arts.athr: Louise Taylor at St James' Park
arts.sum: As a smile spread slowly across his face, Mohamed Salah kissed the club crest adorning his rain sodden red shirt. The Egyptian’s ninth goal in seven Premier League games, and second on the night, had not merely reinforced Liverpool’s title challenge but strengthened the case for offering a forward who, right now, could probably walk unaided on the River Nile, a lucrative contract extension at Anfield. Not that Salah was the only man improving his negotiating position as Fabian Schär’s late equaliser ensured one of the games of the season, if not the decade, ended with honours even as Arne Slot’s league leaders’ winning run was finally halted. arts.ttl: Arsenal's set-piece prowess sees off Man Utd to reduce gap to Liverpool
arts.sum: Arsenal moved within seven points of leaders Liverpool as Jurrien Timber and William Saliba scored from corners to inflict Ruben Amorim's first defeat as Manchester United boss.
